zh.Glamorgan, Wales: 英國威爾斯格拉莫爾根
zh.Priscidwyn, Llan Gefelach, Glamorganshir: 威爾士格拉莫爾根利朗費拉
zh.lifeSummary: 當Margaret verch Gwyn Thomas Howell於大約1440年出生在威爾士格拉莫爾根時，她的父親Hugh Howel21歲，母親Margred Marlos22歲。她於大約1462年與Thomas ap Ieuan在英國威爾斯格拉莫爾根結婚。他們至少是4個兒子和1個女兒的父母。她去世於1470年享年31歲。

zh-hans.Glamorgan, Wales: 英国威尔斯格拉莫爾根
zh-hans.Priscidwyn, Llan Gefelach, Glamorganshir: 威尔士格拉莫爾根利朗費拉
zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Margaret verch Gwyn Thomas Howell于大约1440年出生在威尔士格拉莫爾根时，她的父亲Hugh Howel21岁，母亲Margred Marlos22岁。她于大约1462年与Thomas ap Ieuan在英国威尔斯格拉莫爾根结婚。他们至少是4个儿子和1个女儿的父母。她去世于1470年享年31岁。

nameMeanings.name: verch
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Otto, Orlo.
German: variant of   Ferch  .

variants.name: Ferch
meanings: German: variant of   Ferg  . Compare   Verch  .
Probably also an Americanized form of German Förtsch (see Foertsch ).
Probably also an Americanized form of German Förch (see Foerch ).

nameMeanings.name: Gwyn
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Welsh (Glamorgan) and Irish: variant of   Gwin   or   Gwynne  .

variants.name: Gwin
meanings: Welsh: from gwyn ‘fair, white’, also sometimes ‘white-headed; favorite’. This word was widely used as a personal name in the Middle Ages. It may also be a nickname for a person with fair hair or a noticeably pale complexion. See also Winn .

nameMeanings.name: Thomas
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English, French, Walloon, Breton, German, Dutch, Flemish, Danish, Greek, West Indian (mainly Haiti and Jamaica), and African (mainly Tanzania and Nigeria): from the personal name Thomas, of Biblical (New Testament) origin, from Aramaic t’ōm’a, a byname meaning ‘twin’. It was borne by one of the disciples of Christ, known for his scepticism about Christ's resurrection (John 20:24–29). The Th- spelling is organic, the initial letter of the name in the Greek New Testament being a theta. The English pronunciation as t rather than th- is the result of French influence from an early date. In Britain, the surname is widely distributed throughout the country, but especially common in Wales and Cornwall. In North America, the English form of the surname has absorbed many cognates from other languages (e.g. Assyrian/Chaldean or Arabic Toma and Tuma , Albanian Toma and Thoma , and Slavic surnames listed in 3 below), and their patronymics and other derivatives (e.g. Polish Tomaszewski and Slovenian Tomažič; see Tomazic ). In France, this surname is most common in the Vosges and Brittany. The name Thomas is also found among Christians in southern India (compare Machan , Mammen , and Oommen ), but since South Indians traditionally do not have hereditary surnames, the southern Indian name was in most cases registered as such only after immigration of its bearers to the US. This surname is also very common among African Americans.
Native American (e.g. Navajo): adoption of the English personal name Thomas (see 1 above) as a surname.

Germanized or Americanized form of Polish Tomas , Tomasz, and Tomaś, Sorbian Tomaš (see also 4 below), Croatian Tomaš and Tomas , Slovenian Tomaš and Tomaž, Czech and Slovak Tomáš, all meaning ‘Thomas’.

variants.name: Maas
meanings: Dutch, Flemish, and North German: from a short form of the personal name   Thomas  .

variants.name: Mashak
meanings: Americanized form of Polish Maszak: patronymic from a short form of the personal name Tomasz (see Thomas ) or possibly of a personal name beginning with Ma-. The surname Maszak is very rare in Poland.
In some cases also an Americanized form of Czech Mašek (see Masek ). Compare Mashek .

variants.name: Maschke
meanings: German: from a Slavic-influenced (see below) pet form of the personal names   Thomas   (compare   Maas  ) or   Matthias  .
Germanized form of Sorbian Mašk, Maška, and Maško: from a pet form of the personal name Domaš or Tomaš (see Thomas 3, 4), or of any name beginning with Ma-.
Germanized form of Czech Mašek (see Masek ) and Polish Maszek, both cognates of 2 above.

variants.name: Toma
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Arabic/Muslim Kamal, Nabil, Salem, Kamil, Safa, Samir, Adil, Adnan, Aziz, Bahjat, Basem, Bashar.  Japanese Hiroshi, Takeo, Hideo, Isako, Izumi, Junichi, Kenji, Kiyoko, Masakazu, Rikuo, Sachi, Shigeo.
Romanian and Croatian: from the personal name Toma, an equivalent of Thomas . Compare Tomas .

Italian, French, Polish, Slovak, and Hungarian: from a short, pet, or dialect form of a personal name equivalent to Thomas (Italian Tommaso, French Thomas, Polish Tomasz, Slovak Tomáš, Hungarian Tamás).

variants.name: Mask
meanings: English: unexplained.
German: from a pet form of the personal name   Thomas  .

variants.name: Masino
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Angelo, Ciro, Larraine.
Italian: from a short form of Tommasino, a pet form of thepersonal name Tommaso (see Thomas ).

variants.name: Mascio
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Antonio, Carlo, Gino, Luigi, Paolo, Salvatore.
Italian:
(southern): from a variant of Maso, a short form of the personal name Tommaso (see Thomas ). Alternatively, perhaps from an Italianized form of the Albanian equivalent Mashi.

variants.name: Masel
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Kurt.
German: from a pet form of   Thomas  .

variants.name: Masi
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Angelo, Carmine, Mario, Vito, Carlo, Antonio, Alfonso, Armando, Filomena, Gerardo, Giovanni, Salvatore, Alfonse, Alfonsina, Amato, Anello, Canio, Emilio.
Italian: patronymic or plural form of Maso, a short form of the personal name Tommaso (see Thomas ).

variants.name: Masiello
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Carlo, Rocco, Angelo, Carmine, Franco, Salvatore, Antonio, Carmino, Domenic, Domenica, Francesco, Mauro.
Italian (mainly Naples): from a pet form of the personal name Tommaso (see Thomas ).

nameMeanings.name: Howell
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Welsh and English: in the Welsh border counties, from the Welsh male personal name Hywel ‘eminent’, popular since the Middle Ages particularly in honor of the great 10th-century law-giving Welsh king. The name may also arise from the Breton personal name Houuel, Huwel, Huwal, Howael, cognate with the Welsh name. Bretons were especially numerous in the eastern counties of England. See Powell .
English: perhaps from a Middle English personal name Huwel (spelled Howel), a shortening of Huwelot or Huelin (see Hewlett and Huling ), both pet forms of Middle English Huw (see Hugh ).
English: habitational name from Howell (Lincolnshire), from an uncertain initial element + Old English wella ‘well, spring, stream’.

variants.name: Howle
meanings: English and Welsh:
variant of   Howell  .
variant of   Hole  .

variants.name: Howells
meanings: English and Welsh (Glamorgan): variant of Howell , with genitival or post-medieval excrescent English -s.

English: perhaps occasionally a nickname for someone who was fearless, from Middle English houe, houwe ‘anxiety, fear’ (Old English hogu or hoga) + les ‘less’. If this has survived as a modern surname, its pronunciation has been influenced by that of the name in 1 above.
English: variant of   Holes  .

variants.name: Powell
meanings: Welsh: Anglicized form of Welsh ap Hywel ‘son of Hywel’, a personal name meaning ‘eminent’ (see Howell ).
Irish: mainly of Welsh origin as in 1 above, but sometimes a surname adopted as equivalent of Gaelic Mac Giolla Phóil ‘son of the servant of Saint Paul’ (see Guilfoyle ).
English: variant of   Paul   or   Poole  .

nameMeanings.name: Margaret
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

variants.name: Margareta
meanings: From the Latin form of Greek Margarītēs. See Margaret .

variants.name: Margaretta
meanings: Formed from Margaret.

en.lifeSummary: When Margaret verch Gwyn Thomas Howell was born about 1440, in Glamorgan, Wales, her father, Hugh Howel, was 21 and her mother, Margred Marlos, was 22. She married Thomas ap Ieuan about 1462, in Glamorgan, Wales, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 4 sons and 1 daughter. She died in 1470, at the age of 31.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Margaret verch Gwyn Thomas Howell est née environ 1440, à Glamorgan, Galles, son père, Hugh Howel, avait 21 ans et sa mère, Margred Marlos, avait 22 ans. Elle a épousé Thomas ap Ieuan environ 1462, à Glamorgan, Pays de Galles, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 4 fils et 1 fille. Elle est décédée en 1470, à l’âge de 31 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Margaret verch Gwyn Thomas Howell um 1440 in Glamorgan, Wales geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Hugh Howel 21 und ihre Mutter Margred Marlos 22. Sie hat um 1462 in Glamorgan, Wales, United Kingdom Thomas ap Ieuan geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 4 Söhne und 1 Tochter. Sie starb 1470 im Alter von 31 Jahren.

Lei sposato Thomas ap Ieuan circa 1462, nel Glamorgan, Galles, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 4 figli e 1 figlia. Morì nel 1470, all'età di 31 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Margaret verch Gwyn Thomas Howellが約1440年にウェールズグラモーガンで生まれた時，父Hugh Howelは21歳，母Margred Marlosは22歳であった。約1462年にイギリスウェールズグラモーガンでThomas ap Ieuanと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも4人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。彼女は1470年に31歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Margaret verch Gwyn Thomas Howell(이)가 대략 1440년 웨일스 글램르건에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Hugh Howel(은)는 21세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Margred Marlos(은)는 22세였다. 그녀는 대략 1462년 영국 웨일스 글램르건에서 Thomas ap Ieuan(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1470년 31세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Margaret verch Gwyn Thomas Howell nasceu aproximadamente 1440, em Glamorgan, Gales, seu pai, Hugh Howel, tinha 21 anos e sua mãe, Margred Marlos, tinha 22 anos. Ela casou-se com Thomas ap Ieuan aproximadamente 1462, em Glamorgan, Gales, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 4 filhos e 1 filha. Ela faleceu em 1470, com 31 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Margaret verch Gwyn Thomas Howell родилась около 1440 г. в местности глэморган, Уэльс, её отцу по имени Hugh Howel было 21 год, а матери, которую звали Margred Marlos, 22. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Thomas ap Ieuan около 1462 г. в местности глэморган, Уэльс, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 4 сына и 1 дочь. Она умерла в 1470 г. в возрасте 31 год.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Margaret verch Gwyn Thomas Howell nació aproximadamente 1440, en Glamorgan, Gales, su papá, Hugh Howel, tenía 21 años y su mamá, Margred Marlos, tenía 22 años. Se casó con Thomas ap Ieuan aproximadamente 1462, en Glamorgan, Gales, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 4 hijos y 1 hija. Murió en 1470, a la edad de 31 años.
